response.justification: Bogdan presents a genuinely strong profile with direct Staff-level experience, hands-on AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work in his current role, and credible high-throughput distributed systems background at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests per second, low-latency async I/O). His engineering craftsmanship ethos — TDD, trunk-based development, design patterns — aligns well with the role's technical strategy expectations, and his current position at re:cinq directly involves agentic workflows. The critical blocker is Python: it is entirely absent from his resume, skill list, and all project descriptions despite being a stated hard requirement. The role is worth a conversation only if the hiring team is willing to probe Python readiness directly, as no evidence exists to evaluate it.
strengths: Current role at re:cinq involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, placing him natively in an AI-driven engineering environment
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O, and fiber-based concurrency
Consistent application of engineering craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, design patterns) across multiple senior and staff-level positions
concerns: Python is a hard requirement but is completely absent from the resume, skills section, and all project and employment descriptions
Distributed systems experience at meaningful scale dates primarily to 2018–2020; more recent work has been product-layer and tooling rather than infrastructure at high throughput
No explicit mention of incident response, on-call ownership, or operational readiness, which are called out as required non-technical skills
